Title: Dissection of genetic architecture for glucosinolate accumulations in leaves and seeds of Brassica napus by genome-wide association study

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: associated with seed-specific glucosinolate accumulation; proposed to mediate high leaf/low seed glucosinolate partitioning
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified by GWAS as the candidate gene at the seed-specific locus GSL-A2 for Seed-GSL. 2. It is homologous to Arabidopsis GTR2, a glucosinolate transporter. 3. The paper proposes transporter engineering as a route to breed high leaf/low seed glucosinolate rapeseed, but no direct functional validation was performed here.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS locus-to-candidate inference based on orthology
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field-grown plants in Wuhan, China; leaves sampled at ~90 days after sowing
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: promising target for marker-assisted selection or genome editing to reduce seed glucosinolate while maintaining leaf glucosinolate
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is a candidate gene inferred from GWAS and prior knowledge; direct validation in this paper was not reported.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: altered seed glucosinolate may affect pest and disease resistance indirectly, as discussed for low-GSL cultivars
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant defence; degradation products relevant to human health
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS using Brassica 60K Illumina SNP array; 23,168 SNPs in 521 lines and 23,426 SNPs in 366 lines; metabolite profiling by HPLC for leaf glucosinolates and NIRS for seed total glucosinolate

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: controls methionine-derived side-chain elongation in glucosinolate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: candidate determinant of variation in aliphatic glucosinolate composition, especially 4C/TALI-related variation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS peak on chromosome A3 associated with 4C/TALI identified this tandem MAM homolog cluster. 2. BnaA03g39680D is orthologous to Arabidopsis MAM1. 3. It was proposed to participate in side-chain elongation affecting aliphatic glucosinolate variation in leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS candidate inference based on colocalization and orthology
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field-grown leaves sampled at ~90 days after sowing
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate only; no direct enzyme assay in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS SNP array plus leaf glucosinolate metabolite profiling by HPLC
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant defence; human health-relevant breakdown products
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential target to alter aliphatic glucosinolate composition

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: controls side-chain elongation during aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: candidate contributor to aliphatic glucosinolate variation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified in tandem with two nearby MAM homologs beneath an A3 GWAS peak for 4C/TALI. 2. Proposed to affect side-chain elongation of aliphatic glucosinolates. 3. No direct molecular validation was performed in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS candidate inference based on orthology
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field-grown leaves sampled at ~90 days after sowing
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS SNP array plus HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant defence; human health-relevant breakdown products
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential target to modify glucosinolate side-chain composition

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: controls side-chain elongation during aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: candidate contributor to aliphatic glucosinolate variation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as part of a tandem MAM candidate cluster under an A3 association peak. 2. Orthologous to Arabidopsis MAM1. 3. Proposed to affect leaf aliphatic glucosinolate variation via side-chain elongation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS candidate inference based on orthology
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field-grown leaves sampled at ~90 days after sowing
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS SNP array plus HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant defence; human health-relevant breakdown products

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: involved in biosynthesis of hydroxylated alkenyl aliphatic glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: candidate contributor to OHAlk/TALI variation in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Located ~40 kb upstream from the lead SNP associated with OHAlk/TALI. 2. Orthologous to Arabidopsis GS-OH. 3. Proposed to mediate hydroxylation of alkenyl aliphatic glucosinolates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS candidate inference based on orthology
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field-grown leaves sampled at ~90 days after sowing
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for tailoring specific aliphatic glucosinolate structures

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: involved in biosynthesis of hydroxylated alkenyl aliphatic glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: candidate contributor to OHAlk/TALI variation in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tandem candidate with BnaA03g15200D near the OHAlk/TALI association signal. 2. Orthologous to Arabidopsis GS-OH. 3. Proposed to affect hydroxylated alkenyl glucosinolate accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS candidate inference based on orthology
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field-grown leaves sampled at ~90 days after sowing
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential target to modify hydroxylated glucosinolate profile

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: involved in formation of S-oxygenated aliphatic glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: candidate contributor to S-oxygenated aliphatic glucosinolate formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as ortholog of Arabidopsis FMO-GSOX5. 2. Proposed to catalyze formation of S-oxygenated aliphatic glucosinolates. 3. Candidate inferred from GWAS-supported framework rather than directly validated.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS candidate inference based on orthology
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant defence; cancer-preventive glucosinolate products noted in paper
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: can help enrich health-promoting S-oxygenated glucosinolates

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyses conversion of I3M to 4-hydroxy-indol-3-ylmethyl and/or 1-hydroxy-indol-3-ylmethyl glucosinolate intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: candidate contributor to indolic glucosinolate hydroxylation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as a CYP81F ortholog in the GWAS candidate set. 2. Proposed to hydroxylate indol-3-ylmethyl glucosinolate intermediates. 3. Helps explain variation in indolic glucosinolate traits in leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS candidate inference based on orthology
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential target for tailoring indolic glucosinolate profile

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyses conversion of I3M to hydroxylated indolic glucosinolate intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: candidate contributor to indolic glucosinolate hydroxylation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as a CYP81F ortholog in the GWAS framework. 2. Proposed to catalyze hydroxylation of I3M-derived indolic glucosinolates. 3. Supports the genetic architecture of indolic glucosinolate variation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS candidate inference based on orthology

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyses conversion of I3M to hydroxylated indolic glucosinolate intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: candidate contributor to indolic glucosinolate hydroxylation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as a CYP81F ortholog in the GWAS framework. 2. Proposed to catalyze hydroxylation of indolic glucosinolate intermediates from I3M. 3. Candidate only in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS candidate inference based on orthology

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: aliphatic glucosinolates; total leaf glucosinolates; total seed glucosinolates
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive transcriptional regulation inferred from MYB28 orthology and expression/allelic effects
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthetic program; loss-of-function insertion lowers pathway output
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major locus for high leaf/low seed glucosinolate variation; explains 30.62% of leaf GSL variation in low-seed-GSL panel
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes glucosinolate accumulation, with stronger effect in leaves than seeds; 1356-bp insertion allele lowers both leaf and seed glucosinolate content
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaA03g40190D (BnaA3.MYB28) was validated as the causal gene at GSL-A3. 2. A 1356-bp insertion in exon 2 causes frameshift/premature stop and is associated with significantly reduced leaf and seed glucosinolate contents. 3. The insertion explains 30.62% of leaf glucosinolate variation and 13.51% of seed glucosinolate variation in the low-seed-GSL panel. 4. Expression is significantly lower in lines carrying the insertion. 5. This locus escaped fixation during double-low breeding and is useful for high leaf/low seed glucosinolate improvement.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; resequencing; polymorphism-marker association in 168 low-seed-GSL lines; qRT-PCR expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 366-accession panel; 168-accession low-seed-GSL subset; six extreme accessions for resequencing
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in leaves at ~90 days after sowing; expression is higher in lines lacking the 1356-bp insertion
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field-grown plants in Wuhan, China; low-seed-GSL breeding background is relevant for effect interpretation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: higher glucosinolate may improve pest and disease resistance, while low-glucosinolate breeding may increase susceptibility
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: high-value marker/gene for breeding rapeseed with high leaf glucosinolate and low seed glucosinolate; suitable for marker-assisted selection and metabolic engineering
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not fixed during double-low rapeseed breeding, unlike other MYB28 loci under selection; amino acid sequence is 99.7% similar to BrA03MYB28 (Bra012961)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant defence; human health-related breakdown products
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS SNP array; HPLC metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR; targeted resequencing; GenBank accession numbers MN103513-MN103518

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: aliphatic glucosinolates; total leaf glucosinolates; total seed glucosinolates
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive transcriptional regulation inferred from MYB28 orthology
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major common locus with the largest effect on both leaf and seed glucosinolate
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes total glucosinolate accumulation in leaves and seeds
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GSL-A9 was the largest-effect common locus for leaf and seed glucosinolate. 2. Candidate gene is BnaA09MYB28, a MYB28 homolog. 3. This locus was co-selected and nearly fixed in low-seed-GSL breeding lines.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: selection for low seed glucosinolate likely reduced this allele frequency; lower GSL may compromise defence
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: important major-effect regulator, but largely fixed in double-low materials
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate gene reported absent in low-glucosinolate accessions according to cited prior work
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant defence; human health-related breakdown products

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive transcriptional regulation inferred from MYB28 orthology
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major common locus controlling seed glucosinolate and also associated with several leaf aliphatic traits
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Candidate gene for common locus GSL-C2-2. 2. Associated strongly with Seed-GSL and also with 4OHB, 5C and OHAlk in leaves. 3. Co-selected and nearly fixed during double-low breeding.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: low-GSL selection may reduce defence-related glucosinolate accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: major regulator but less useful in double-low germplasm because largely fixed
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate gene reported absent in low-glucosinolate accessions according to cited prior work
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant defence; human health-related breakdown products

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: aliphatic glucosinolates; total leaf glucosinolates; total seed glucosinolates
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive transcriptional regulation inferred from MYB28 orthology
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major common locus contributing to both leaf and seed glucosinolate variation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes glucosinolate accumulation in leaves and seeds
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Candidate gene for common locus GSL-C7. 2. Associated with Seed-GSL in both panels and Leaf-GSL in the 366-line panel. 3. Likely co-selected during double-low breeding.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: major regulator but largely fixed in low-seed-GSL materials
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate in bracket based on ZS11 reference genome
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant defence; human health-related breakdown products

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: aliphatic glucosinolates; total leaf glucosinolates; total seed glucosinolates
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive transcriptional regulation inferred from MYB28 orthology
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major common locus for both leaf and seed glucosinolate levels
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Candidate gene for common locus GSL-C9-1. 2. Associated with both Leaf-GSL and Seed-GSL. 3. Like other major MYB28 loci except A3, it appears to have been co-selected during double-low breeding.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: lower glucosinolates may reduce pest and disease defence
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: major-effect regulator, but breeding utility in double-low lines may be limited due to fixation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant defence; human health-related breakdown products

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive transcriptional regulation inferred from MYB34 orthology
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: candidate regulator of TIND and I3M traits
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. One of two loci associated with TIND and I3M. 2. Candidate gene is homologous to Arabidopsis MYB34, a positive regulator of indolic glucosinolate biosynthesis. 3. Identified by GWAS as part of the indolic glucosinolate regulatory architecture.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS locus-to-candidate inference based on orthology
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential target to increase indolic glucosinolates for defence
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate only; no direct validation in this paper

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive transcriptional regulation inferred from MYB34 orthology
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: candidate regulator of TIND and I3M traits
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as one of two candidate genes for loci associated with TIND and I3M. 2. Homologous to Arabidopsis MYB34. 3. Proposed positive regulator of indolic glucosinolate biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS locus-to-candidate inference based on orthology
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential target to enhance indolic glucosinolate-mediated defence
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate only; no direct validation in this paper
